cases.title: Testing-plan QR token behavior row: 30-second TTL issuance and rotation while session Open
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Class session is in Open state per testing-plan §3.1 QR token behavior coverage block.
Server clock is stable for TTL boundary assertions.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and capture qrPayload, expiresAt, and tokenState.
Assert expiresAt minus meta.timestamp is approximately 30 seconds and tokenState is Valid.
Wait until after expiresAt and call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current again without closing the session.
Compare qrPayload and expiresAt between the two responses.
Verify session state remains Open throughout the rotation cycle.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan QR token behavior row: each issued token is valid for 30 seconds, rotates to a new valid token while session stays Open, and display metadata refreshes without session reopen per AC-02 and BR-03.

cases.title: M04 QR Orchestrator persists token bound to one session with expiresAt = issuedAt + 30s
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for qr_session_tokens table.
An Open class session row exists with known sessionId.
M04 issueToken(sessionId) is callable through service layer.
steps: Invoke M04 issueToken(sessionId) for the Open session.
Query qr_session_tokens and assert exactly one new Valid row bound to sessionId with issued_at and expires_at populated.
Assert expires_at equals issued_at plus 30 seconds using server time.
Advance harness clock beyond TTL and invoke issueToken or getCurrentToken again.
Assert prior token evaluates as Expired and newly persisted token differs with fresh 30-second window.
cases.expected: M04 module + repository integration issues QRSessionToken rows bound to exactly one classSessionId with deterministic 30-second TTL per SM-02 and FR-11; rotation creates new token records without reactivating expired tokens.

cases.title: SM-02 token Valid requires SM-01 session Open; close transitions tokens to Invalid
preconditions: Scheduled session fixture exists with assigned Lecturer.
Session control (M03) and QR orchestrator (M04) share the same database.
Token validation path is wired to session-state guard.
steps: Attempt M04 issueToken on Scheduled session and record rejection or absence of Valid token.
Execute open transition (Scheduled to Open) and verify first Valid token is issued with expiresAt.
Record beforeState Valid and invoke close transition (Open to Closed).
Query token rows and assert remaining Valid tokens are Invalid or fail validation through session guard.
Attempt token resolution for previously issued qrPayload and assert rejection without attendance success.
cases.expected: beforeState Scheduled blocks Valid token issuance; Open enables Valid with 30s TTL; after Open to Closed, tokens are Invalid per SM-02/TMI-02 and cannot be used for successful check-in.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current returns 200 envelope with Valid token metadata
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with Authorization bearer token.
Target session is currently Open.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current with Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error} with error null.
Assert data contains classSessionId matching path param, tokenState Valid, expiresAt ISO-8601 UTC, and non-empty qrPayload.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp.
Re-call endpoint after one TTL rollover and confirm HTTP 200 with refreshed expiresAt and updated qrPayload.
cases.expected: Endpoint returns HTTP 200 with stable envelope schema per API design §4.4; qr/current data exposes Valid tokenState, 30-second expiry metadata, and opaque-or-URL qrPayload resolvable server-side.

cases.title: Lecturer authenticates via login then fetches current QR token for assigned Open session
preconditions: Lecturer credentials exist for POST /v1/auth/login.
An Open session exists in a section assigned to that Lecturer.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with Lecturer credentials and capture accessToken from HTTP 200 envelope.
Send GET /v1/me with bearer token and assert Lecturer identity and assigned section scope.
Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current with bearer token.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.tokenState Valid, data.qrPayload present, and data.classSessionId matches the assigned Open session.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken; GET /v1/me confirms Lecturer scope; subsequent qr/current succeeds with Valid rotating token metadata for the assigned Open session only.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance reflects Open session with active QR rotation context
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Open session with enrollments exists and QR tokens are actively rotating.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and confirm tokenState Valid with qrPayload.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization.
Validate HTTP 200 and envelope shape.
Assert data.state is Open, counts object includes present/late/pending/rejectedAttempts keys, and rows array reflects enrolled roster shape per API design §4.5.
Wait one TTL cycle, re-fetch qr/current, and confirm attendance endpoint still reports Open with unchanged roster contract.
cases.expected: Attendance roster endpoint returns HTTP 200 with data.state Open and live roster payload while FR-11 QR tokens remain actively issued and rotatable for the same session.

cases.title: Primary lifecycle flow: open session triggers first rotating QR token bound to session
preconditions: Lecturer account and Scheduled session fixture are available.
Session has no prior openedAt timestamp.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer.
Assert response data.state Open with qr preview containing expiresAt and qrPayload.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and verify tokenState Valid bound to the same sessionId.
Decode or resolve qrPayload server-side and assert it maps to the opened classSessionId without trusting client-supplied session IDs.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled student with resolved token before expiry and confirm outcome is not SessionNotOpen.
cases.expected: WF-01 / E2E-01 segment: session open commits Open state and system issues first Valid QR token bound to exactly one class session with 30-second TTL; token is resolvable for check-in while session remains Open.

cases.title: Alternate flow: continuous QR rotation across multiple TTL cycles without session close
preconditions: Open session with active QR rotation is available.
Assigned Lecturer and enrolled student accounts exist.
Harness can observe at least two consecutive token generations.
steps: Capture token T1 from GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current.
Wait past T1 expiresAt and capture token T2 from the same endpoint while session remains Open.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with T1 after its expiry and record ExpiredQr rejection.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with T2 before its expiry as enrolled student.
Fetch T3 after T2 expiry and confirm rotation continues with session still Open.
cases.expected: Expired token from prior cycle is rejected; newly rotated valid token supports check-in in the same Open session; rotation continues every ~30 seconds without lecturer reload or session reopen per FR-11 auto-refresh behavior.

cases.title: Student, ITAdmin, and out-of-scope Lecturer are denied qr/current token fetch
preconditions: Student, ITAdmin, and Lecturer-not-assigned-to-section test accounts exist with valid tokens.
An Open session exists in a section the out-of-scope Lecturer is not assigned to.
Assigned Lecturer token is available as positive control.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as Student.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as ITAdmin.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as out-of-scope Lecturer.
Read HTTP status and error.code for each denied request (expect 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope).
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as assigned Lecturer and assert HTTP 200 with Valid token metadata.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as AcademicAdmin and assert override access per API design §4.4 roles.
cases.expected: Unauthorized roles cannot retrieve QR token metadata; assigned Lecturer and AcademicAdmin may fetch current QR per permission matrix; no qrPayload leakage to denied actors.

cases.title: QR current endpoint rejects or omits Valid token when session is not Open
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Session fixtures exist in Scheduled and Closed states.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{scheduledSessionId}/qr/current for a Scheduled session.
Record HTTP status and whether data.tokenState is absent or non-Valid.
Open a separate session, then close it; call GET /v1/class-sessions/{closedSessionId}/qr/current.
Assert response does not return tokenState Valid with active qrPayload for Closed session.
Verify no new Valid QRSessionToken rows are created for non-Open sessions.
cases.expected: FR-11 restricts issuance to Open sessions only: Scheduled and Closed targets do not expose active Valid rotating QR metadata; API returns appropriate error or non-Valid state without issuing new tokens.

cases.title: Token bound to different session is rejected; only current-session token resolves
preconditions: Two Open sessions (sessionA and sessionB) exist in different sections with distinct rotating tokens.
Enrolled student is enrolled only in sessionA section.
steps: Capture qrPayload tokenB from GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionB}/qr/current.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as student enrolled in sessionA using tokenB.
Record HTTP status and error.code (invalid-session or MalformedQrToken equivalent).
Capture tokenA from sessionA qr/current and submit check-in with tokenA.
Verify failed attempt for tokenB is logged with structured reason and tokenA attempt is not rejected for wrong-session.
cases.expected: FR-11 enforces one-session binding (TMI-01): token from sessionB cannot satisfy check-in for sessionA; server resolves token to classSessionId without trusting client session hints per BR-04.

cases.title: Concurrent qr/current fetches during rotation return consistent Valid token for Open session
preconditions: Open session exists with QR rotation scheduler active.
Integration harness can dispatch parallel GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current requests.
Assigned Lecturer auth context is established.
steps: Fire multiple parallel GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current requests at a fixed instant before TTL boundary.
Collect all responses and assert each returns HTTP 200 with tokenState Valid for the same sessionId.
Assert all responses share the same qrPayload and expiresAt for the current rotation window.
Repeat parallel fetches immediately after TTL rollover and confirm all responses converge on the next valid token without corrupt state.
cases.expected: Concurrent reads during a rotation window return consistent current token metadata; post-TTL parallel reads converge on the next Valid token without duplicate-active Valid rows violating SM-02 invariants.

cases.title: Lecturer PG-05 QrDisplayPanel auto-refreshes QR before expiry without manual reload
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
An Open session is displayed on PG-05 session control page with QrDisplayPanel and QrCountdownRing visible.
Session name, section code, and Open status badge are rendered.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId} (PG-05) and observe QrDisplayPanel with high-contrast QR image and countdown or refresh indicator.
Note the visible countdown value and QR image fingerprint or payload preview.
Wait past one 30-second TTL cycle without refreshing the browser tab.
Observe QrCountdownRing reset and QR image crossfade or swap to a new token before prior expiry.
Verify session badge remains Open, session name and section code stay visible, and no full-page reload occurred.
Confirm refreshed QR remains scannable for student check-in flow.
cases.expected: Browser journey demonstrates FR-11 and FR-14 projection-friendly display: QR refreshes automatically before 30-second TTL expires via QrDisplayPanel/QrCountdownRing without lecturer manual reload, maintaining Open-state indicators throughout rotation.
